commands_executed: echo "Password expiration detected. Please resolve this prerequisite issue before proceeding with remediation. The system requires immediate password change which is blocking all command execution. You must either: 1) Log in interactively and change your password, or 2) Use 'chage -d 0 <username>' to force password expiration, then log in to set a new password. Once resolved, I can proceed with setting dcredit = -1 in /etc/security/pwquality.conf to enforce digit requirements in passwords."

llm_verdict.message:
I cannot proceed with the remediation because the system requires an immediate password change due to password expiration. All command execution is blocked by this prerequisite issue.

**Required action before remediation:**
The system is displaying: "You are required to change your password immediately (password expired). WARNING: Your password has expired. Password change required but no TTY available."

You must first resolve the password expiration issue by:
1. Logging in interactively and changing the password, OR
2. Using `chage -d 0 <username>` to force password expiration, then logging in to set a new password

Once the password expiration is resolved, I can proceed with the planned remediation to set dcredit = -1 in the pam_pwquality configuration to enforce digit requirements in passwords.

per_turn.command: echo "Password expiration detected. Please resolve this prerequisite issue before proceeding with remediation. The system requires immediate password change which is blocking all command execution. You must either: 1) Log in interactively and change your password, or 2) Use 'chage -d 0 <username>' to force password expiration, then log in to set a new password. Once resolved, I can proceed with setting dcredit = -1 in /etc/security/pwquality.conf to enforce digit requirements in passwords."
